content: KILL ZONES: London Kill Zone: 2:00-5:00 AM EST. New York Kill Zone: 7:00-10:00 AM EST. These are when institutional traders are most active and volume peaks. Trade ONLY during kill zones. Avoid Asian session (lower volume, more manipulation). First 15 minutes of each kill zone = ANALYSIS (mark levels, identify bias). Execute in the MIDDLE of the kill zone when structure is clear.

SESSION HIGH/LOW LEVELS: Mark previous day high (PDH), previous day low (PDL), Asian session high, Asian session low. During London open: price often sweeps Asian high/low then reverses. During NY open: price often sweeps London high/low or PDH/PDL. The sweep + immediate reversal = entry signal. Don't predict the sweep — wait for it to happen and confirm with reversal candle.

SQUEEZE BREAKOUTS: Price compresses between clear support/resistance, building energy. LONGER squeeze = BIGGER breakout. Volume increases before breakout. Enter on candle close above/below squeeze range. Stop at opposite end. Target = size of squeeze projected from breakout. Use 15m/1h for identification, 5m for entry. False breakouts: wait for RETEST of broken level before entering.

SESSION TRADING RULES: Wait 15 minutes for session to establish range. Mark session high/low. Trade breakouts from session range during kill zones. If price sweeps session low and reverses with bullish candle → long. If sweeps session high and reverses with bearish candle → short. 5m for entries, 1h for bias. Don't trade lunch hours (11:30 AM - 1:30 PM EST) — low volume, choppy, stop hunting.

ORDER BLOCK + FVG CONFLUENCE: When an order block aligns with a fair value gap at the same price level = HIGH-PROBABILITY entry zone. Order block provides institutional support, FVG provides the gap price wants to fill. Enter when price reaches zone with confirmation candle (rejection wick or engulfing). Stop below order block. Target POC or next structure level.

RISK MANAGEMENT (Juvier): Risk 0.5-1% per trade. Max 3 trades/day. 2 consecutive losses = stop for day. Always use stop loss. Trail to break-even after 1R. Partial profits at 1:1 (50%), rest to 1:2 or 1:3. Only trade during kill zones. Don't trade 30 min before/after major news (NFP, CPI, FOMC). Wait for dust to settle, then trade the reaction.

DISPLACEMENT CANDLES: Large, full-bodied candles with small or no wicks that move price aggressively through a level. Displacement shows INTENT — someone big is buying or selling. When you see displacement in one direction, expect continuation. A displacement candle that breaks structure (BOS) is a strong entry signal. The bigger the displacement, the stronger the conviction. Displacement at a key level (S/R, order block) is the highest probability setup.

BREAKER BLOCK ENTRIES: A breaker block is an order block that has been BROKEN — price closed through it. The broken level flips from support to resistance or vice versa. Breakers are powerful because trapped traders from the original order block are now underwater. When price returns to a breaker, expect a strong reaction. Entry: wait for price to reach breaker + rejection candle. Stop beyond the breaker. Target next structure level.

ASIAN SESSION RANGE: Asian session (7 PM - 2 AM EST) typically forms a tight range. This range is CRITICAL for London session trading. Mark Asian high and low. During London open: price often sweeps one side of the Asian range, traps traders, then reverses. The sweep of Asian high/low during London kill zone is one of the most reliable setups in forex/crypto. Entry on the reversal candle after the sweep.

LONDON vs NEW YORK: London session = higher probability for REVERSALS (sweeps of Asian range). New York session = higher probability for CONTINUATIONS (of London move) or REVERSALS (if significant news). London/NY OVERLAP (8-10 AM EST) = highest volume window of the day. Best time for breakout trades. After NY open, watch for the first 15-minute candle — it often sets the direction for the rest of the session.

NEWS TRADING: Don't trade 30 minutes before major news releases (NFP, CPI, FOMC, ECB). Spreads widen, stops get hunted, slippage increases. AFTER the news: wait for the initial spike, then the retracement, then trade the SECOND move. The first move is emotional; the second move is institutional. If news causes a sweep of a key level + immediate reversal = highest probability entry.

SESSION-SPECIFIC RULES: ASIAN SESSION: only trade if you see a clear range forming. Mark high/low. LONDON SESSION: look for sweeps of Asian range. Enter on reversal. NY SESSION: look for continuation of London move OR reversal if significant news. NY REVERSAL: if London was bullish and NY opens with bearish displacement, expect reversal. Always wait for confirmation — don't front-run.
